rules: RULE-01: Input. Apply scenario.input.actions in order. scenario.input.constants is OPTIONAL; any top-level key it omits falls back to the defaults in src/constants.js. At start turn=0, gameStatus='PLAYING'; ONLY the WAIT action increments turn.

RULE-02: WAIT action -> turn += 1, then run exactly one full tick: src/systems.tick applies every subsystem step in the fixed ORDER (power, oxygen, water, food, population, morale, research, economy, hazards, grid, thermal, radiation, hull, maintenance, comms, cargo, crew, navigation, lifesupport, derive). Subsystem behavior is defined by those existing modules and MUST stay byte-for-byte unchanged.

RULE-03: BUILD action with field target in {solar,farm,recycler} -> if credits >= constants.build[target]: credits -= cost, that capacity level += 1, append 'BUILT '+target to log; otherwise append 'BUILD_FAILED '+target. BUILD does NOT change turn and runs NO tick.
RULE-04: RATION action -> toggle the rationing flag and append 'RATION ON' or 'RATION OFF' to log. RATION does NOT change turn and runs NO tick.

RULE-05: Evaluate the end check at scenario start (BEFORE any action) AND immediately AFTER each action: if population <= 0 set gameStatus='LOST' and append 'STATION_LOST'; else if research >= constants.research.goal set gameStatus='WON' and append 'MISSION_COMPLETE'. The end check only fires while gameStatus is 'PLAYING'.
RULE-06: Once gameStatus != 'PLAYING' (WON, LOST or EVACUATED), stop immediately — process no further actions from the scenario sequence; the current state is final.

RULE-07: EVACUATE action -> set gameStatus = 'EVACUATED' and stop immediately, exactly like RULE-06. EVACUATE changes NOTHING else — turn, every resource, every subsystem field, alerts and log stay exactly as they were the instant before the EVACUATE; EVACUATE runs no tick, appends no log line and pushes no alert. A scenario that never EVACUATEs prints exactly as before (regression must be byte-identical).

RULE-08: gameStatus is exactly one of the literal strings 'PLAYING', 'WON', 'LOST', 'EVACUATED'. alerts and log are string arrays kept in firing order; alerts collects per-tick subsystem warnings and log collects discrete events.

files.description: CLI entry point. Parses --scenario N, runs the engine, prints the final state via format.render.

Deterministic action loop: dispatches WAIT/BUILD/RATION to actions.apply, runs the end check before and after each action, and (this card) handles EVACUATE as an immediate halt. This is the ONLY module this card edits.
